author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

title: Chapter 75

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Thus indeed it was fitting that Scripture should be fulfilled.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But in you I grieve for this, that you have shown yourselves worthy to fulfill the part of wickedness."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: I might rather say, O wretched traditors! if I were minded, or rather if justice urged me to cast up against all of you the deeds of some among your number.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But as regards what bears on all of you, O wretched heretics, I on my part will quote the remainder of your words; for it is written, "There must be also heresies among you, that they which are approved may be made manifest among you."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Therefore "it was fitting thus that Scripture should be fulfilled.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But in you I grieve for this, that you have shown yourselves worthy to fulfill the part of wickedness."
